sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The click at (51, 12) seems to have moved the purple square one step up the path. Looking at the images, the purple square is now slightly higher and to the right compared to before. Let me verify this and continue moving it along the path toward the blue circle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check where the purple square is now
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
purples = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'p' and n['pixels'] == 9]
for p in purples:
    rows = [b[0] for b in p['boundary']]
    cols = [b[1] for b in p['boundary']]
    center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
    print(f"Purple center: {center}")

# Path positions
path = [(57, 6), (55, 8), (53, 10), (51, 12), (49, 14), (47, 16), (45, 18), (43, 20), (41, 22), (39, 24), (37, 26), (35, 28), (33, 30), (31, 32), (29, 34), (27, 36), (25, 38), (23, 40), (21, 42), (19, 44), (17, 46)]
# Blue circle center is at (15, 48)

# If square moved to (51, 12), next is (49, 14), etc.
# Let me batch the remaining clicks along the path
# Current position should be (51, 12), need to reach near blue circle at (15, 48)
# Path from index 3 to end: (49,14) to (17,46)

# Let's check if the square actually moved
if purples:
    cur = purples[0]
    rows = [b[0] for b in cur['boundary']]
    cols = [b[1] for b in cur['boundary']]
    center = (sum(rows)//len(rows), sum(cols)//len(cols))
    print(f"Current position: {center}")
    
    # Find which path index this is closest to
    best_idx = 0
    best_dist = float('inf')
    for i, (r, c) in enumerate(path):
        d = abs(r - center[0]) + abs(c - center[1])
        if d < best_dist:
            best_dist = d
            best_idx = i
    print(f"Closest path index: {best_idx} ({path[best_idx]})")
    
    # Batch remaining path clicks
    remaining = path[best_idx+1:]
    print(f"Remaining positions: {remaining}")
    
    # Click all remaining positions in batch
    actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in remaining]
    action(actions)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
